ReceivedSalami.title: ReceivedSalami
ReceivedSalami.description: Represents a record of salami money received by a user during Eid celebrations, as part of the Eid Salami Counter feature.

id.description: Unique identifier for the ReceivedSalami entity.

userId.description: Reference to the User who received this salami. (Relationship: User 1:N ReceivedSalami)

fromPerson.description: The name of the person from whom the salami was received. This field is optional.

receivedAt.description: The date and time when this salami entry was logged.

EidSelfie.title: EidSelfie
EidSelfie.description: Represents an Eid selfie photo uploaded by a user for the Eid Selfie Contest.

id.description: Unique identifier for the EidSelfie entity.

userId.description: Reference to the User who uploaded this selfie. (Relationship: User 1:N EidSelfie)

imageUrl.description: The URL where the uploaded selfie image is stored.

caption.description: A user-provided caption for the Eid selfie. This field is optional.

likesCount.description: The total number of likes this selfie has received. This count can be denormalized for performance or calculated from SelfieLike entities.

uploadedAt.description: The date and time when the selfie was uploaded.

SelfieLike.title: SelfieLike
SelfieLike.description: Represents a like given by a user to an Eid selfie, used for tracking likes and preventing duplicate likes in the Eid Selfie Contest.

id.description: Unique identifier for the SelfieLike entity.

selfieId.description: Reference to the EidSelfie that was liked. (Relationship: EidSelfie 1:N SelfieLike)

userId.description: Reference to the User who gave the like. (Relationship: User 1:N SelfieLike)

likedAt.description: The date and time when the like was recorded.

definition.description: Stores individual records of salami money received by a specific user. This is private data, owned by the user specified in the path and the document's 'userId' field. Includes denormalized 'userId' for authorization independence.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user who received the salami.

params.name: salamiId
params.description: The unique identifier for a specific received salami entry.

definition.description: Stores all submitted Eid selfies for the contest. Selfies are publicly viewable for the leaderboard, but write access is restricted to the uploading user. Includes denormalized 'userId' for authorization independence.

params.name: selfieId
params.description: The unique identifier for a specific Eid selfie.

definition.description: Stores individual 'likes' given to a specific Eid selfie. Each like is owned by the user who created it. Includes denormalized 'userId' (the liker) and 'selfieId' for authorization independence.

params.name: selfieId
params.description: The unique identifier of the Eid selfie being liked.

params.name: likeId
params.description: The unique identifier for a specific like.

firestore.reasoning:
The proposed Firestore structure prioritizes Authorization Independence and Structural Segregation to ensure secure, scalable, and easily debuggable security rules. For user-specific private data like `ReceivedSalami`, a hierarchical path-based ownership model (`/users/{userId}/receivedSalami/{salamiId}`) is used. Each `ReceivedSalami` document explicitly includes the `userId` field, denormalizing the owner's ID directly into the document. This eliminates the need for `get()` calls to parent documents in security rules, allowing atomic write operations and simplifying authorization checks to `request.auth.uid == resource.data.userId` or `request.auth.uid == userId` from the path. This structure directly supports QAPs by enabling secure list operations where users can only query their own salami records using a path like `/users/{request.auth.uid}/receivedSalami`.

For publicly viewable and collaborative data like `EidSelfie` entries, a top-level collection (`/eidSelfies/{selfieId}`) is employed. Each `EidSelfie` document contains the `userId` of the uploader, ensuring that ownership for modifications is self-contained without parent dependencies. This collection can be read by all authenticated users for the leaderboard, with write access restricted to the `userId` associated with the document. The `likesCount` field is denormalized to facilitate efficient leaderboard sorting. The `SelfieLike` entity, representing a user's 'like' on a selfie, is implemented as a subcollection (`/eidSelfies/{selfieId}/likes/{likeId}`). Each `SelfieLike` document includes both `userId` (the liker) and `selfieId` (the liked selfie). This denormalization ensures that a like document can be authorized independently, checking `request.auth.uid == resource.data.userId` to prevent unauthorized modification or deletion of other users' likes, while also explicitly linking it to the parent selfie without requiring a `get()` operation. This approach effectively handles QAPs for the selfie contest by allowing secure public reads of selfies for display and a clear, explicit path for users to create their own likes.
